description_long: This object is a rustic, circular guard rail constructed from thick, cylindrical wooden posts connected by two horizontal wooden rails. The posts are evenly spaced and appear to be roughly hewn, with visible wood grain and a natural brown finish. The rails are attached to the posts with a simple joinery method, giving the structure a sturdy, utilitarian appearance. The overall form is open and low, designed to demarcate a boundary or protect a small area without fully enclosing it. There are no visible fasteners or decorative elements, emphasizing its functional, handcrafted style. The ends of the rails are blunt and terminate at the posts, with no additional treatment or capping.
description: A circular, rustic wooden guard rail made of thick posts and horizontal rails, finished in natural brown. The structure is open, sturdy, and utilitarian, with visible wood grain and blunt rail ends.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular arrangement of thick wooden posts and rails, highlighting the open entrance and the even spacing of the posts.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the depth of the guard rail and the alignment of the posts, with the rails clearly visible connecting each post.
description_view_2: From this perspective, the circular shape is more pronounced, and the blunt ends of the rails at the posts are visible.
description_view_3: This view shows the continuity of the rails and the consistent spacing of the posts, with the open area in the center clearly defined.
